title: Malty Pillowy Argentinian Ikura Empanadas
description: These empanadas are a cozy twist on traditional Argentinian cuisine, with a malty, pillowy texture and a pop of ikura for added flavor.

ingredients.name: ikura
ingredients.notes: ikan roe, halal-friendly and sustainably sourced

instructions: Combine flour, warm water, yeast, and salt in a large mixing bowl.
Mix until a shaggy dough forms, then let rest for 10 minutes.
Add malt syrup and mix until dough comes together in a ball.
Knead dough for 5-7 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place dough in a greased bowl, cover with plastic wrap, and refrigerate for at least 1 hour.
Divide dough into 8 equal pieces, then roll out each piece into a thin circle.
Place 1 tablespoon of ikura in the center of each circle, then fold dough over to form a half-moon shape and press edges together to seal.
Place empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving 1 inch of space between each empanada.
Brush tops of empanadas with beaten egg and bake in a preheated oven at 37575f (19075c) for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
Remove empanadas from oven and immediately submerge in an ice bath to stop cooking and help retain texture.
Let empanadas cool completely on a wire rack before serving.
